title: Complementary health associate professionals
description: Conduct research in the genetics, nutrition, reproduction, growth, and development of domestic farm animals. Use chemistry, microbiology, engineering, and other sciences to study the principles underlying the processing and deterioration of foods; analyze food content to determine levels of vitamins, fat, sugar, and protein; discover new food sources; research ways to make processed foods safe, palatable, and healthful; and apply food science knowledge to determine best ways to process, package, preserve, store, and distribute food. Conduct research in breeding, physiology, production, yield, and management of crops and agricultural plants or trees, shrubs, and nursery stock, their growth in soils, and control of pests; or study the chemical, physical, biological, and mineralogical composition of soils as they relate to plant or crop growth. May classify and map soils and investigate effects of alternative practices on soil and crop productivity. (Based on 39 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Fit patients for orthopedic braces, prostheses, or supportive devices, such as crutches.
Apply finger and hand pressure to specific points of the body.
